title: What Americans Need to Understand About China
summary: Ezra Klein discusses Xi Jinping’s China with former Australian Prime Minister Kevin Rudd. The two cover how Xi has doubled down on communist ideology and significantly consolidated his own power while transforming China into an industrial juggernaut unrivaled in the world.

title: China Is Sabotaging the World That Enables Its Rise
summary: Following state visits by Trump and Putin to Beijing in May, Chinese state media emphasized the symbolism of foreign leaders paying tribute to Xi Jinping rather than concrete outcomes. The authors argue this reflects Beijing's narrative that China has surpassed its rivals and reclaimed centrality on the world stage.

title: China exports its problems
summary: China's economy grew just 4.3 percent in the second quarter, missing official targets, as traditional sectors like construction shrink rather than merely slow. Fierce competition in emerging industries such as batteries and robotics are squeezing profits, a phenomenon known domestically as "neijuan." An economist at Tsinghua University estimates real unemployment near 10 percent, far above the official 5 percent...

title: This Satellite Imagery reveals just how advanced China's military is
summary: Satellite imagery analyzed by the Telegraph reveals China constructing detailed replicas of US Navy vessels, including an Arleigh Burke class destroyer, in the remote Taklamakan desert. Though 2,700 kilometers from the sea, the models feature masts and radar equipment closely matching the originals. Analysts say the structures let Beijing rehearse missile strikes with precision, perhaps in preparation to...

title: Georgia official discusses Trump's claims about election security
summary: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks with Gabriel Sterling, of the Georgia Secretary of State's Office, about President Trump's claims about election security.

title: Trump said the election system is broken. What do swing voters make of the claim?
summary: What did some swing state voters think of President Trump's primetime speech on elections?

title: $600,000 for a presidential pardon? Inside the growing industry of clemency lobbying
summary: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks with NOTUS reporter Jasmine Wright about the growing lobbying industry to get presidential pardons under the second Trump administration.

title: Politics chat: How much faith can you have in the country's election systems?
summary: President Trump and Secretary of Homeland Security Markwayne Mullin make claims about the vulnerability of election systems in the U.S., but how true are those claims?

title: Cuban artist and dissident exiled from country after 5 years in prison arrives in US
summary: A famous Cuban dissident artist and musician, Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country.

title: Why permanent daylight saving time has failed in the US before

title: 'Rare' La Niña may form in the Atlantic at the same time as this year's 'super' El Niño
summary: While conditions are heating up for a "super" El Niño in the Pacific, the inverse may be happening in the Atlantic.

title: My client lost her life savings to a romance scammer. Then she got a tax bill.

title: Proposals to Limit Member of Congress Financial Activities: Analysis of Introduced Legislation in the 119th Congress
summary: In recent years, some Members of Congress have proposed reforms that would prohibit the purchase, sale, ownership, or holding of certain financial instruments by Members of Congress and other specified congressional officers and employees. title: Military Service Records, Awards, and Unit Histories: A Guide to Locating Sources
summary: This guide provides information on locating military unit histories and individual service records of discharged, retired, and deceased military personnel. It also provides information on locating and replacing military awards and medals. Included is contact information for military history centers, websites for additional sources of research, and a bibliography of other publications, including related CRS reports.

title: Pooling Europe's compute
summary: Frontier AI training has needed compute at one site, hard in Europe given power, chip and political limits. RAND Europe finds distributed training eases the power and political constraints and pools Europe's scattered chips, but adds no new chips.

title: The ICC Has Real Flaws. Dismantling It Without an Alternative Is Not the Answer.
summary: Secretary of State Rubio’s campaign offers no alternative for prosecuting genocide and war crimes that would otherwise go unpunished. Decades of bipartisan U.S. policy have recognized that an imperfect court is better than none.

title: Maintaining America's Leadership in Biomedical Innovation

title: Teachers’ Unions Are Losing One of Their Government Perks
summary: State lawmakers are limiting the special advantages that let teachers’ unions lobby on the public dime. title: This July 4, at 250 Years, Let Us Rededicate Ourselves to Protecting Our Elections
summary: America has much to celebrate this July 4, but also much to do. Even after 250 years, the task of conserving the Republic given to us by our Founding Fathers is a never-ending struggle. Thomas Jefferson, the author of our Declaration of Independence, outlined the responsibility of citizens: The price of liberty is not just the blood of past...

title: Supreme Court Must Rein in D.C. Court of Appeals

title: Blaming Trump for the Latest Outbreak of Ebola Is Absurd
summary: The Trump administration is again coming under fire for last year’s dismantling of the U.S. Agency for International Development. Why now? title: Expanding Technical and Professional Components to all Medicare Physician Fee Schedule Services
summary: Revising Medicare’s allocation of indirect practice expenses to depend on both physician work and clinical labor for all services would align payments between services with and without separately billable professional and technical components.

title: Technical Documentation for the Thirteenth American School District Panel Survey
summary: This report provides technical information about the spring 2026 survey of the American School District Panel. The authors describe the survey administration and weighting processes they used to produce nationally representative estimates.

title: Shrinking Budgets, Fewer Students
summary: This report presents findings from the spring 2026 American School District Panel survey. Budget shortfalls and declining enrollment have risen to the top of district leaders’ concerns, worsening other challenges that districts are facing.

title: Blind Spot Indicators for Space
summary: In this report, the authors present methodologies for synthesizing space-related research and development efforts by the United States and China to project trends in future capabilities and operations that use space.

title: Syria’s State Sponsor of Terrorism Designation
